Article Title: Fresh bid to impeach CEC Gyanesh Kumar: Opposition submits new notice signed by 73 MPs in Rajya Sabha
Article Text:
Gyanesh Kumar

Watch Gyanesh Kumar Under Fire: Opposition Moves To Remove Chief Election Commissioner After SIR Row Gyanesh Kumar Under Fire: Opposition Moves To Remove Chief Election Commissioner After SIR Row

NEW DELHI: Opposition parties on Friday submitted a fresh notice in Rajya Sabha seeking a motion to remove Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar. The notice has been signed by 73 MPs.Congress leader Jairam Ramesh alleged that there are nine specific charges against the CEC on the ground of 'proven misbehaviour' and called his continuation as an assault on the Constitution.In a post on X, he wrote: "73 Opposition MPs in the Rajya Sabha have just submitted to its Secretary General a fresh Notice of Motion for presenting an Address to the President of India praying for the removal of Shri Gyanesh Kumar, Chief Election Commissioner of India, on the ground of proven misbehaviour constituted by acts and omissions committed on and after 15 March 2026, within the meaning of Article 324 (5) of the Constitution of India read with Article 124(4) thereof, Section 11(2) of the Chief Election Commissioner and Other Election Commissioners (Appointment, Conditions of Service and Term of Office) Act, 2023 and the Judges (Inquiry) Act, 1968. There are now nine specific charges against the CEC that have been documented in great detail and that simply cannot be denied or whitewashed away. His continuation is an assault on the Constitution. It is an absolute disgrace that the man continues to be in office to do the bidding of the PM and HM."Earlier, Trinamool Congress (TMC) MP and spokesperson Derek O'Brien on Monday said that the opposition would move a fresh impeachment notice against the CEC with additional charges.“19 political parties and around 300 MPs had earlier moved an impeachment notice against the CEC. In the coming days, a fresh impeachment notice with additional charges will be brought,” O’Brien told reporters at a press conference in Kolkata.Also Read | Oppn to bring fresh motion to remove CEC Gyanesh Kumar, says TMC's Derek O'Brien; alleges BJP planning 'conspiracy' in Bengal Earlier this month, notices submitted in both Houses of Parliament seeking Kumar’s removal, were rejected by Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla and Rajya Sabha Chairman, Vice President CP Radhakrishnan.Opposition parties have accused Kumar of 'partial conduct,' alleging that he favoured the ruling BJP, particularly in connection with the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls. The earlier notice, backed by over 193 MPs from multiple opposition parties, cited several charges, including 'proved misbehaviour' and concerns over his handling of electoral roll revisions in states such as Bihar and West Bengal.The move comes amid heightened political tensions as first phase of West Bengal assembly elections was conducted on Thursday, where disputes over electoral roll revisions and administrative decisions by the Election Commission have become key flashpoints.Under Article 324(5) of the Constitution, the process to remove the Chief Election Commissioner is similar to that for a Supreme Court judge. It requires a special majority in Parliament and can only be initiated on grounds of proven misbehaviour or incapacity. Once admitted, a committee comprising senior judicial members and a distinguished jurist examines the charges and the CEC is given an opportunity to present a defence before any further proceedings.
Article Summary: 73 Opposition MPs have submitted a fresh notice in Rajya Sabha seeking a motion to remove Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar. Congress leader Jairam Ramesh alleged that there are nine specific charges against the CEC. The move comes amid heightened political tensions as first phase of West Bengal assembly elections was conducted on Thursday.

Article Text:
Election Commission

EC official threatened

KOLKATA: EC suspended Sandeep Sarkar, officer in-charge of Hingalganj PS, over allegations of partisanship and failure to ensure a level playing field during the assembly polls. It directed disciplinary action be initiated against him, reports Sanjib Chakraborty.The decision comes amid heightened political tension in North 24 Parganas. over allegations that BJP distributed cash to voters ahead of polling.Police detained two BJP netas — Tarun Mondal and former Assam MLA Kishore Nath — who were allegedly caught distributing money among residents. BJP, though, dismissed the charges as baseless, claiming that police acted under the influence of TMC members.Cops initiated a probe into a complaint alleging that a group of party workers threatened EC officials and snatched poll documents when they were conducting home-based voting for an elderly citizen in Kalighat, near the CM’s home.
Article Summary: EC suspended Sandeep Sarkar, officer in-charge of Hingalganj PS, over allegations of partisanship and failure to ensure a level playing field during the assembly polls. Decision comes amid heightened political tension in North 24 Parganas.

Article Title: Opposition files new notice seeking CEC Gyanesh Kumar's ouster
Article Text:
.

Watch Gyanesh Kumar Under Fire: Opposition Moves To Remove Chief Election Commissioner After SIR Row Gyanesh Kumar Under Fire: Opposition Moves To Remove Chief Election Commissioner After SIR Row

NEW DELHI: Opposition MPs in Rajya Sabha on Friday submitted to the secretary general a fresh notice seeking to move a motion to remove Gyanesh Kumar as chief election commissioner on the ground of “proven misbehaviour”.Earlier this month, notices submitted by opposition MPs in both LS and RS were rejected by the speaker and the chairman, respectively. In its fresh notice, opposition has levelled nine new charges against Kumar.According to Congress spokesperson Jairam Ramesh, a motion has been sought “on the ground of proven misbehaviour” — one of the instances being the “Straight Talk” social media post by EC after a meeting with a TMC delegation on April 8. The meeting had ended on an acrimonious note, with TMC leaders saying the CEC asked them to “get lost”, and EC accusing them of shouting.“There are now nine specific charges against the CEC that have been documented in great detail and that simply cannot be denied or whitewashed away. His continuation is an assault on the Constitution. It is an absolute disgrace that the man continues to be in office to do the bidding of PM and HM,” Ramesh said.The motion has been signed by MPs from Congress, TMC, DMK, SP, RJD, CPI, CPM, NCP(SP), Shiv Sena (UBT), JMM, IUML, NC and AAP.As part of the fresh charges, opposition has accused Kumar of “continued partisan asymmetry in enforcement of model code of conduct”, wherein it is alleged that EC failed to act on complaints against PM Narendra Modi’s address to the nation on April 18 on the defeated Constitution amendment bill related to women’s reservation amid assembly polls. CEC “has issued no show-cause notice, no advisory, and no public response” to any of the complaints made regarding this, it said.Other charges include “administrative lapses evidencing institutional proximity to the governing party” and “conduct unbecoming of a constitutional functionary”. The list of charges also includes mass disenfranchisement in West Bengal involving deletion of around 91 lakh voters and judicial exclusion of around 34 lakh voters from the franchise.
Article Summary: Opposition MPs in Rajya Sabha on Friday submitted to the secretary general a fresh notice seeking to move a motion to remove Gyanesh Kumar as chief election commissioner. Earlier this month, notices submitted by opposition MPs in both LS and RS were rejected by the speaker and the chairman. In its fresh notice, opposition has levelled nine new charges against Kumar.

Article Title: 'Bewildering contradictions': Kiren Rijiju slams Rahul Gandhi over 'BJP will win West Bengal remarks'
Article Text:
Watch Mamata Banerjee Targets BJP, Alleges Political Appointments And Signals Delhi Push Mamata Banerjee Targets BJP, Alleges Political Appointments And Signals Delhi Push

NEW DELHI: Union minister Kiren Rijiju on Friday criticised Rahul Gandhi over his comments on West Bengal politics, accusing the Congress leader of taking contradictory positions on the upcoming state election.Reacting to Rahul Gandhi’s remarks on social media, Rijiju wrote on X: "Bewildering.... on the one hand Rahul Gandhi Ji is saying only Congress can defeat BJP in West Bengal and on the other hand he's saying, due to TMC's polarizing politics, BJP will win West Bengal election!"The criticism came after Rahul Gandhi targeted Mamata Banerjee and her All India Trinamool Congress, despite both parties being part of the INDIA opposition bloc.In a post on X, Rahul said: “If Mamata ji had run a clean govt, and had she not polarised West Bengal, there would have been no way for BJP to make inroads into Bengal.”Appealing to voters to support Congress, he added: “In this battle of ideologies, only Congress can defeat BJP.”Rahul Gandhi also accused the Bharatiya Janata Party of undermining democratic institutions.“BJP steals elections. SIR is their way to steal elections.There is a direct fight between Congress and BJP’s ideologies,” he said in a video message.He further alleged that the BJP was working against Bengal’s identity and diversity.“BJP works against West Bengal’s culture, history, and Bengali language. BJP netas say ‘one nation, one language, one religion’. In this country there are many languages, religions, and ideologies. While we are trying to protect the Constitution, BJP is trying to destroy it,” Rahul said.The exchange highlights growing tensions within the INDIA bloc as campaigning intensifies in West Bengal, where Congress and the Trinamool Congress remain political rivals despite being part of a broader anti-BJP alliance nationally.
Article Summary: Kiren Rijiju criticises Rahul Gandhi over his comments on West Bengal politics. He accuses the Congress leader of taking contradictory positions on the upcoming state election. The criticism came after Rahul Gandhi targeted Mamata Banerjee and her All India Trinamool Congress. Both parties are part of the INDIA opposition bloc.

Article Title: West Bengal Election: 'Can’t change orders every day': SC refuses to hear pleas of poll staff excluded from voter rolls in Bengal
Article Text:
Image used for representative purposes

Watch From Corruption To Citizenship: How TMC & BJP Fought The SIR-Changed Bengal Polls From Corruption To Citizenship: How TMC & BJP Fought The SIR-Changed Bengal Polls

NEW DELHI: The Supreme Court on Friday declined to entertain pleas seeking its intervention over allegations that certain officials deputed for election duty were excluded from voter rolls in the upcoming West Bengal Assembly elections.A bench headed by Chief Justice Surya Kant told the petitioners to approach the appropriate appellate tribunal, observing that the court could not “change its orders every day.”“Please raise the problem before the Appellate Tribunal. We can’t change our orders every day,” the CJI said during the hearing, as quoted by ANI.However, during the proceedings, Justice Joymalya Bagchi observed that regardless of whether they are able to vote in the current elections, the more significant issue of their continued inclusion in the electoral rolls would be examined by the court.Counsel representing the petitioners argued that even individuals engaged in election duty are entitled to vote, prompting the court’s observation that the matter should be pursued through the prescribed legal mechanism.The court also granted liberty to persons excluded from the voter roll in the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) exercise in West Bengal elections to approach the Chief Justice of the Calcutta High Court with their grievance.A bench led by CJI Surya Kant further directed Appellate Tribunals to grant out-of-turn hearings in cases of excluded persons whose appeals are pending, particularly where urgency is demonstrated. The court said, “As regards those names who have been excluded in SIR and those who have filed appeals before the Appellate Tribunal, the Tribunal may grant them out of turn hearing of appeals, especially to appellants who are able to prove urgency. ”The court noted that most issues had already been addressed in its April 13 order and observed, “We can understand that issues may arise on day to day basis.”It further clarified that petitioners and stakeholders may approach the Chief Justice of the Calcutta High Court either administratively or judicially, depending on the nature of the grievance.
Article Summary: The Supreme Court on Friday declined to entertain pleas seeking its intervention over allegations that certain officials were excluded from voter rolls. A bench headed by Chief Justice Surya Kant told the petitioners to approach the appropriate appellate tribunal, observing that the court could not “change its orders every day”

Article Title: Pawan Khera: Passport row: Gauhati HC rejects Congress leader Pawan Khera's anticipatory bail plea
Article Text:
Pawan Khera

Watch Pawan Khera Faces Setback As SC Stays Bail, Cites Jurisdiction Issue In Assam CM Passport Row Case Pawan Khera Faces Setback As SC Stays Bail, Cites Jurisdiction Issue In Assam CM Passport Row Case

Poll What is your stance on the involvement of the Assam government in this case? They are justified in pursuing the case They should not be involved

NEW DELHI: The Gauhati high court on Friday rejected Congress leader Pawan Khera’s anticipatory bail plea in a case filed by Riniki Bhuyan Sharma, wife of Assam chief minister Himanta Biswa Sarma The FIR stems from Khera’s allegations that Sharma held multiple foreign passports and undisclosed overseas assets. During the previous hearing, Khera’s counsel, led by Abhishek Manu Singhvi, argued that he was not a flight risk and that arrest was unnecessary, calling the case politically motivated.The Assam government, represented by Advocate General Devajit Lon Saikia, opposed relief, arguing the case involved serious offences including cheating and forgery, not merely defamation. The court had earlier reserved its order after extensive arguments from both sides.The Telangana High Court had earlier granted him a seven-day transit anticipatory bail, but the Assam Police moved the Supreme Court against it.
Article Summary: The Gauhati high court on Friday rejected Congress leader Pawan Khera’s anticipatory bail plea. The case was filed by Riniki Bhuyan Sharma, wife of Assam chief minister Himanta Biswa Sarma. The court had earlier reserved its order after extensive arguments from both sides.

Article Title: Census will list caste, says Amit Shah, rules out Muslim women's quota
Article Text:
Watch 'Never Be Forgiven': PM Modi Dares Opposition On Women Quota Bill, Priyanka Gandhi Questions Timing 'Never Be Forgiven': PM Modi Dares Opposition On Women Quota Bill, Priyanka Gandhi Questions Timing

New Delhi: Union home minister Amit Shah seized on Samajwadi Party MP Dharmendra Yadav's demand for quota for OBC and Muslim women to hammer home his party's avowed opposition to reservation based on religion.He also reiterated govt's stand that the caste of citizens will be mentioned during the ongoing census to reject SP chief Akhilesh Yadav's claim that Centre's decision to link women's quota to the 2011 Census is driven by its reluctance for the same. Opposing the introduction of the Constitution amendment bill, Dharmendra said in Lok Sabha that his party will never support women's reservation till a separate provision for quota for OBC and Muslim women is made in the law. "Dharmendra Yadav has made an unconstitutional demand. Our Constitution does not allow reservation based on religion at all. I will reiterate govt's resolve that giving reservation to Muslims on the basis of religion is unconstitutional," Shah said, amplifying his party's opposition to faith-based quotas on the ground that British acquiescence to the Muslim League's demand for a separate electorate for Muslims had encouraged calls for the creation of Pakistan.When Akhilesh stepped in to ask if Muslim women are not part of half of the country's population, Shah said he has no objection if SP gives all its tickets to Muslims.He said house listing is being done as part of Census 2027, and caste will be counted next. "Houses have no castes. If SP has its way, then even houses will have castes."
Article Summary: Union home minister Amit Shah seized on Samajwadi Party MP Dharmendra Yadav's demand for quota for OBC and Muslim women. He reiterated govt's stand that the caste of citizens will be mentioned during the ongoing census. Shah said he has no objection if SP gives all its tickets to Muslims.

Article Title: Bengaluru techie, his family arrested after fiancée dies by suicide
Article Text:
A Bengaluru-based techie and his parents were arrested by the Chinna Chowk police in Kadapa district after the girl, who was supposed to get married to Shahjahan in Aug this year, died by suicide.

TIRUPATI: A Bengaluru-based techie and his parents were arrested by the Chinna Chowk police in Kadapa district after the girl, who was supposed to get married to Shahjahan in Aug this year, died by suicide.According to Chinna Chowk SI Prathap Reddy, the girl, Rehana, got engaged to Shahjahan in Feb this year and they were scheduled to get married in Aug. While Rehana and Shahjahan got along well initially and used to have long conversations over the phone, his behaviour suddenly changed about three weeks ago and he began pressurising Rehana to call off the marriage.According to the girl's father Nadendla Raja, Shajahan reportedly told Rehana that he already had a relationship with four other women and that he wasn't getting any "feelings" for her.Stating that he agreed to the wedding because he was being pressurised by his parents, he asked her to call off the marriage.Rehana, who slipped into a depression, committed suicide by hanging herself from the ceiling at her house in Patel Road locality of Kadapa on April 21.She left behind a 12-page suicide note, in which she explained her ordeal and the manner in which she was driven to take the extreme step by Shahjahan.Based on a complaint lodged by her father Nadendla Raja, police registered a case and arrested Shahjahan and his parents, M Veeraiah and M Waheeda from Proddatur, on charges of abetment to suicide. The accused were produced before the local court and sent to judicial remand.
Article Summary: Rehana, who slipped into a depression, committed suicide by hanging herself from the ceiling at her house in Patel Road locality of Kadapa on April 21. Shahjahan and his parents, M Veeraiah and M Waheeda from Proddatur, were arrested on charges of abetment to suicide.

Article Title: Taking steps to free Hindu monk Chinmoy Das: Bangladesh minister
Article Text:
.

Watch Bangladesh PM Tarique Rahman Makes BIG Promise To Hindus As India Detains Hindu Cop Killer In Delhi Bangladesh PM Tarique Rahman Makes BIG Promise To Hindus As India Detains Hindu Cop Killer In Delhi







DHAKA: Bangladesh cultural affairs minister Nitai Roy Chowdhury on Friday assured leaders of the Hindu community that “appropriate steps” would be taken to ensure the early release of Hindu monk Chinmoy Krishna Das, who faces charges of sedition and murder.The Tarique Rahman-led govt is actively working to preserve the cultural heritage of minority communities while carrying out “necessary investigations”, Chowdhury, a prominent Hindu BNP leader and senior advocate, said without elaborating on the investigations being carried out.Chinmoy was arrested under the erstwhile Muhammad Yunus-led interim govt under seven charges in 2024, with a court on Thursday granting him bail in a land grabbing case. “I will raise the matter in the appropriate forum within the next few days and take necessary measures for his early release,” Chowdhury said at an event.
Article Summary: Chinmoy Krishna Das was arrested under the erstwhile Muhammad Yunus-led interim govt under seven charges in 2024. A court on Thursday granted him bail in a land grabbing case. “I will raise the matter in the appropriate forum within the next few days and take necessary measures for his early release,” Chowdhury said at an event.
